chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Types of Hajj

text: 2550 - وَ رَوَى إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ يُفْرِدُ اَلْحَجَّ فَيَطُوفُ بِالْبَيْتِ وَ يَسْعَى بَيْنَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ ثُمَّ يَبْدُو لَهُ أَنْ يَجْعَلَهَا عُمْرَةً فَقَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ لَبَّى بَعْدَ مَا سَعَى قَبْلَ أَنْ يُقَصِّرَ فَلاَ مُتْعَةَ لَهُ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2550 - Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar narrated from Abu Basir:
I said to Abu ‘Abdullah (as), "A man intends to perform Ifrad Hajj (individual Hajj), performs Tawaf around the Ka‘bah, and walks between Safa and Marwah. Then, he decides to turn it into ‘Umrah. What should he do?"
Imam (as) replied: "If he pronounced Talbiyah (the devotional call) after completing the Sa‘i and before trimming his hair, then he cannot perform Tamattu‘."
